evals.name: basic-pipeline-usage
evals.prompt: I've got a folder of Claude Code session logs at ./mylogs (jsonl files in the same shape as ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl). I want to turn them into a SFT training file at ./train.jsonl, redact any secrets, and only keep decent-quality conversations. Walk me through what to run.
evals.expected_output: Agent should recommend `python -m logsnatch run --source claude --input ./mylogs --output ./train.jsonl`, mention that intermediate _raw/_redacted/_cleaned/_scored files will be written, and explain --min-score (default 0.5) for quality filtering.

evals.name: add-codex-parser
evals.prompt: I want to add a parser for OpenAI Codex CLI logs to this tool. They live at ~/.codex/sessions/*.jsonl, one JSON object per line, with fields {role, content, tool_calls, timestamp}. What files do I need to create or change, and roughly what's the shape of the parser class?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) say to create logsnatch/parsers/codex.py subclassing BaseParser with SOURCE='codex' and DEFAULT_LOG_DIR=Path.home()/'.codex'/'sessions'; (2) implement discover_sessions (glob) and parse_session returning {id, source, messages}; (3) register in logsnatch/parsers/__init__.py REGISTRY; (4) add a test in tests/test_parsers.py. Should NOT recommend modifying cli.py or pipeline stages.

evals.name: debug-validate-failures
evals.prompt: I ran the pipeline and `validate` says 30 of my 200 records are failing the chat template. How do I figure out what's wrong and fix it without re-running the whole pipeline from scratch?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) suggest inspecting the failing records in the _cleaned.jsonl intermediate (kept by `run`); (2) point at common causes — orphaned tool_call_id, empty assistant content, missing tools array; (3) suggest re-running just the affected stage (e.g., redact or clean) on the existing intermediate rather than starting over; (4) mention --output on validate to save passing records.
